provenance.source_corpus: Recovery of a Wild Fish Population from Whole-Lake Additions of a Synthetic Estrogen

nodes.name: Whole-lake additions of an active component of the birth control pill (17 α-ethynylestradiol; EE2)

source_spans.text: Whole-lake additions of an active component of the birth control pill (17 α-ethynylestradiol; EE2) that resulted in the collapse of the fathead minnow (Pimephales promelas ) population.
Whole-lake additions of an active component of the birth control pill (17 α-ethynylestradiol; EE2) that resulted in the collapse of the fathead minnow (Pimephales promelas ) population.
VTG was detected in male fathead minnow at concentrations up to 22 200-fold higher than fish from reference lakes or pretreatment fish.

Delayed spermatogenesis and reproductive failure of the fathead minnow were observed in the experimental lake the following year, precipitating a near extinction of the species that persisted for two years post-treatment.
By 3 years post-treatment, whole-body vitellogenin concentrations in male fathead minnow had returned to baseline, and testicular abnormalities were absent.
determine if complete recovery was possible.

nodes.name: wastewater treatment facilities that reduce discharges of estrogens

source_spans.text: These results suggest that wastewater treatment facilities that reduce discharges of estrogens and their mimics can improve the health of resident fish populations in their receiving environments.
These results suggest that wastewater treatment facilities that reduce discharges of estrogens and their mimics can improve the health of resident fish populations in their receiving environments.
